title: AVP Conformance Test Case (v0.1)
description: Language-agnostic test case format. Each SDK implements a thin harness (see HARNESS.md) that reads cases of this shape, runs them against its implementation, and asserts. Cases describe (a) the Commission the agent receives, (b) a deterministic script of model responses, tool outputs, and resolver outcomes, and (c) expectations over the emitted event trajectory.

id.description: Stable identifier. SHOULD match the file name (without .json).

title.description: One-line summary of what this case asserts.

description.description: Optional longer prose. Cite the specific spec ambiguity or requirement this case pins down.

spec_refs.description: Section refs into the v0.1 specs (trajectory.md, commission.md, agent-descriptor.md, resolver.md) or schema fragments this case verifies.

applies_to.description: Which side(s) of the seam this case validates. Most cases test agent behavior given a scripted resolver; supervisor cases assert the supervisor produces conforming Commissions.

scripted_only.description: When true, this case requires a scripted-model harness (deterministic model responses via `scripted_model`). Live harnesses that drive a real LLM MUST skip it — the wire rule it tests can't be reliably triggered with a live model (e.g. forcing a refusal, forcing the model to invoke a specific tool name). The AVPAgent reference harness still runs these cases.

scripted_model.description: One entry per turn the harness should serve in order. The harness's mock model returns these responses to the agent regardless of conversation history.

scripted_tools.description: Map from tool name to the response the harness returns when that tool is called locally. Tools not listed here either fall through to a Commission-managed MCP server (when one is declared) or surface as tool_failed (unknown tool).

omit_resolver.description: When true, the harness skips wiring a ResolverDriver even if the Commission carries managed assets. Used to exercise the `resolver_not_configured` startup gate (spec/v0.1/resolver.md §2).

scripted_resolver.description: Configures the in-process ResolverDriver the harness wires when the Commission carries any managed assets. Cases that omit this with a non-empty Commission asset list exercise the managed_ref_resolve_failed path (every lookup misses).

resolutions.description: Maps lookup key → outcome. Key may be '<kind>:<id>' (e.g. 'mcp_server:github') or just '<id>' (matched across kinds for short cases). Matching value carries either {result: ...} for success or {error, error_code?} for the resolve-failed path.

subagent_spawns.description: Maps Commission.subagents[].id to a canned avp.spawn_subagent outcome. Only consulted when the model invokes a subagent at runtime.

agent_descriptor.description: Optional. AgentDescriptor the harness passes to AVPAgent so it emits the run_requested + agent_described prelude. Cases that don't set this exercise the no-prelude path (the agent skips emission and the trajectory looks the same as before this field existed). The value is validated as a Pydantic AgentDescriptor at run time.

agent_builtin_tools.description: Optional. Declares the agent's built-in tool catalog so cases can exercise the agent-internal contribution to agent_started.tools[]. Each entry is a tool descriptor the agent exposes natively (e.g., bash, read_file).

events.description: Ordered patterns the trajectory must contain (see 'ordering').

ordering.description: in_order_subsequence: matches must appear in this order, but unmatched events MAY interleave. in_order_strict: matches must be the entire trajectory in order, no extras. any_order: matches must each appear somewhere.

forbidden_events.description: Patterns that MUST NOT appear anywhere in the trajectory.

reasoning_blocks.description: Reasoning / thinking blocks the model produced this turn. Each becomes one avp.reasoning_emitted event.

refusal.description: When set, the agent emits avp.refusal_recorded and terminates with StopReason.refused. Universal across providers — `reason` is the provider's verbatim code (anthropic 'refusal'/'sensitive', openai 'content_filter', gemini 'SAFETY'/'BLOCKLIST'/etc.).

converged.description: When true and tool_calls is empty, the agent should treat the response as terminal and stop with reason=converged.

result.description: Material the resolver returns to the agent. Shape varies by kind (per spec/v0.1/resolver.md §3).

SubagentSpawnSpec.description: Canned outcome for one avp.spawn_subagent call, mirroring agent.drivers.SubagentSpawnOutcome. The harness emits subagent_invoked carrying child_run_id, then emits subagent_returned (or subagent_failed when `error` is set).

child_run_id.description: Child run_id stamped on subagent_invoked.data['avp.subagent.run_id']. When omitted, the harness synthesizes a deterministic id.

text.description: Result text the subagent returned to the parent (becomes the parent's tool_result-equivalent).

structured.description: Optional structured result (any JSON value). Surfaces on subagent_returned.data.avp.subagent.result.structured.

reason.description: How the subagent terminated; mirrors AVP StopReason.

usage.description: Subagent's RunStateSnapshot rollup. Reflected on subagent_returned.data.avp.subagent.usage AND added to the parent's cumulative state.

error.description: When set, the harness emits subagent_failed instead of subagent_returned with this error message.

EventMatcher.description: Partial-match pattern. Every key/value in 'match' must appear in the event with that exact value (deep-equal for nested objects). Other keys MAY be present.

label.description: Human-readable name for assertion failure messages.
